steps.title: Set up the matching condition
steps.content: A linear equation in one variable has infinitely many solutions only when, after distributing and combining like terms, the coefficient of the variable on the left equals that on the right AND the constant terms on each side are equal. Both conditions must hold simultaneously, giving two equations in the two unknowns $a$ and $b$.

steps.title: Solve for a and b
steps.content: Applying the coefficient-match equation determines $a = 2$, and substituting that into the constant-match equation determines $b = 7$. The pair $(a, b) = (2, 7)$ matches choice B.
